stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 30 patients. The sample mean is 304.3 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 88.5 mmHg. However, 3 of the 30 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 27 observations have a mean of 282.2 mmHg and standard deviation of 63.9 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 304.3, 95% CI = [271.2, 337.4]. Without outliers: mean = 282.2, 95% CI = [257.6, 306.8]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [257.6, 337.4]. Point estimate: 293.2 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [257.6, 337.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
